title: Golden Firmware Image Compliance Across NDFC Fabrics
exclusions: Intent-configuration **`complianceStatus`** drift versus declared VLAN/VRF/network objects (see UC-18.4.2 — distinct controller plane); **NDI** anomaly/AIOps narratives lacking **`image_policy_compliance`** pulls (UC-18.4.1); multi-vendor upgrade-wave spreadsheets normalized outside **NDFC** **`imagePolicy`** bindings (UC-18.3.13); **APIC** firmware MO governance without LAN-fabric **`imagepolicies`** (cat-18.1.*); standalone **NX-OS** estates with no **Nexus Dashboard Fabric Controller** Software Image Management (**SIM**) enrollment; legacy **DCNM**-only workflows without **NDFC 12.x** Image Management application parity.
dataSources: `index=cisco_dc` / `index=cisco_network` / `index=network_operations` blending **`sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:imagemanagement`** JSON from **`GET https://<nd-cluster>/appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/imagemanagement/imagepolicies`** plus **`GET …/imagemanagement/policies/{policyId}/devices`** for **`image_policy_compliance`** (**In-Sync**, **Out-of-Sync**, **Failed**, **NA**) — **`GET …/lan-fabric/rest/control/fabrics/{fabric}/inventory`** (**`cisco:ndfc:fabric`**) — **`GET …/imagemanagement/imageupgrades`** upgrade-history (**60–300s** polls) — **`NDI`** **`GET https://<nd-cluster>/sedgeapi/v1/site_status/imageManagement`** rollup (**`cisco:ndi:health`**) — **NDFC** syslog **`facility=local6`** lines bearing **`IMAGE-MGMT-INFO`** / **`IMAGE-MGMT-FAIL`** MNEMONICs — optional **gRPC** telemetry **`image-version`** exports stamped **`cisco:nxos:telemetry`** beside **`Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus`** (**Splunkbase 6876**) — **`cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk`** (**Splunkbase 7777**).
app: **`cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk`** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) ships **NDFC** image-governance tiles; pair with **`Splunk REST API Modular Input`** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) polling **`/appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/imagemanagement/**` tree with **ND** **Bearer** tokens; **`Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus`** (**6876**) for **NX-OS** syslog/telemetry corroboration.
spl:
(index=cisco_dc OR index=cisco_network OR index=network_operations OR index=cisco_operations)
(sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:imagemanagement" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:fabric" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndi:health")
earliest=-24h@m
| eval device_name=trim(coalesce(device_name, switch_name, hostname, deviceName, devicename))
| eval fabric_name=trim(coalesce(fabric_name, fabricName, fabric, nd_fabric, siteName))
| eval image_policy_name=trim(coalesce(image_policy_name, imagePolicyName, policyName, policy_name))
| eval compliance_state=trim(coalesce(compliance_state, image_policy_compliance, policyCompliance, status))
| eval target_version=trim(coalesce(target_version, image_policy_target_version, policyTargetVersion, golden_target))
| eval running_version=trim(coalesce(running_version, nxos_version, software_version, runningImage, imageVersion))
| eval device_role=lower(trim(coalesce(device_role, role, switch_role, node_role, "unknown")))
| eval ndi_lane=if(sourcetype="cisco:ndi:health" AND (match(lower(_raw),"imagemanagement|image.?policy") OR isnotnull(image_policy_compliance)),1,0)
| eval insync=if(compliance_state="In-Sync",1,0)
| eval oos=if(compliance_state="Out-of-Sync",1,0)
| eval failed=if(compliance_state="Failed",1,0)
| eval na_sw=if(compliance_state="NA",1,0)
| eval push_fail=if(match(lower(_raw),"image.?push.*fail|rollback|IMAGE-MGMT-FAIL") OR (sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:imagemanagement" AND match(lower(coalesce(eventName,event_name,"")),"fail|rollback")),1,0)
| eval issu_step=lower(trim(coalesce(installer_step, installerStep, workflowStep, "")))
| eval issu_gate=if(match(issu_step,"dryrun|dry.?run"),"dryrun", if(match(issu_step,"commit"),"commit", if(match(issu_step,"rollback"),"rollback", "other")))
| eval vpc_peer=trim(coalesce(vpc_peer_name, vpcPeer, peer_switch, peer_hostname, ""))
| where isnotnull(device_name) AND (isnotnull(fabric_name) OR ndi_lane=1 OR match(sourcetype,"ndfc"))
| eval version_key=mvindex(split(running_version,"("),0)
| eventstats dc(device_name) AS devices_in_fabric BY fabric_name
| eventstats sum(insync) AS fabric_in_sync BY fabric_name
| eval fabric_compliance_pct=round(100*fabric_in_sync/nullif(devices_in_fabric,0),2)
| eventstats latest(running_version) AS peer_running latest(target_version) AS peer_target BY fabric_name vpc_peer
| eval vpc_drift=if(isnotnull(vpc_peer) AND vpc_peer!="" AND peer_running!=running_version AND fabric_name=fabric_name,1,0)
| stats latest(_time) AS last_seen,
        latest(fabric_compliance_pct) AS fabric_compliance_pct,
        latest(target_version) AS target_version,
        latest(running_version) AS running_version,
        latest(device_role) AS device_role,
        latest(image_policy_name) AS image_policy_name,
        latest(compliance_state) AS compliance_state,
        latest(issu_gate) AS issu_gate,
        sum(push_fail) AS image_push_fail_events,
        max(vpc_drift) AS vpc_peer_drift,
        values(compliance_state) AS compliance_mix
    BY fabric_name device_name
| eval straggler_flag=if(compliance_state="Out-of-Sync" OR compliance_state="Failed",1,0)
| eval severity_lane=case(compliance_state="Failed", "failed_policy",
    compliance_state="Out-of-Sync" AND vpc_peer_drift=1, "vpc_pair_drift",
    compliance_state="Out-of-Sync", "golden_straggler",
    compliance_state="NA", "policy_gap",
    1=1, "watch")
| sort fabric_name straggler_flag - image_push_fail_events
| head 400
description: Monitors Cisco **NDFC** **12.4** Image Management SIM via **imagePolicy** REST `/appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/imagemanagement/imagepolicies` and `…/policies/{policyId}/devices`, reporting **image_policy_compliance** In-Sync, Out-of-Sync, Failed, or NA before mixed **NX-OS** trains widen defects. Covers **imageManagement** lifecycle Image Selected → Pre-Upgrade Validation → Pre-ISSU Compatibility → Image Push → Reload → Post-ISSU Verification; **ISSU** gates **installer.dryRun**/**installer.commit** with rollback; **validateImage** REST; template-bound **imagePolicy** assignment; trains LTS, Maintenance, Long-Term-Support; roles **leaf-image**, **spine-image**, **border-leaf-image**. Ingest merges `…/imagemanagement/imageupgrades` polls (60–300s), **policy:upgrade:install**, **NDI** `site_status` **imageManagement**, **facility=local6** **IMAGE-MGMT-*** syslog, **cisco:ndfc:imagemanagement**, **cisco:ndfc:fabric**, **TMG** **Nexus 9000** matrix—not CSV drift lists.
value: **NDFC** **`imagePolicy`** timelines expose firmware drift while **`validateImage`** and **ISSU** gates still permit orderly staged reloads—Splunk joins **`image_policy_compliance`**, **`validateImage`** outcomes, **`imageupgrades`** job telemetry, **TMG** matrix expectations, and **NDI** fabric rollups so CAB-backed waves retire **Out-of-Sync** stragglers before emergency upgrades collide with interoperability defects.
implementation: Land **`rest_ta`** pollers (**60–300s**) on **`imagepolicies`**, **`policies/{policyId}/devices`**, **`fabrics/{fabric}/inventory`**, **`imageupgrades`**, optional **`validateImage`** probes—stamp **`cisco:ndfc:imagemanagement`**/**`cisco:ndfc:fabric`**, ingest **`NDI`** **`site_status`** **`imageManagement`** as **`cisco:ndi:health`**, normalize **`FIELDALIAS`** for **`device_name`**/**`fabric_name`**/**`compliance_state`**, wire Splunkbase **7777** fabric tokens. **Quick win:** schedule **`GET …/imagepolicies`** + **`…/devices`** every five minutes before layering **`validateImage`** or **ISSU** parsers.
visualization: (1) **Single value + trend** — **`fabric_compliance_pct`** (**devices In-Sync / total**) per **`fabric_name`** token. (2) **Table** — **`device_name`**, **`image_policy_name`**, **`compliance_state`**, **`running_version`**, **`target_version`**, **`device_role`** highlighting **Out-of-Sync** stragglers. (3) **Horizontal funnel / stage tracker** — **`installer.dryRun`** → **`installer.commit`** → reload/post-check counts per ISSU wave. (4) **Timechart** — **`image_push_fail_events`** rate per **`fabric_name`** weekly. (5) **Alert table** — **`vpc_peer_drift`** pairs where **`running_version`** diverges inside the same **`fabric_name`**. (6) Optional heat strip — **`severity_lane`** (**failed_policy**, **vpc_pair_drift**, **golden_straggler**, **policy_gap**).
schema: CIM **Inventory** (**device**, **host**, **`fabric_name`**, **`device_role`**) plus **Updates** (**package**/patch identifiers mapped from **`running_version`** versus **`target_version`**, compliance posture mirrored into update workflow semantics).
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate **Inventory** once **`device_name`** + **`fabric_name`** stabilize for **`tstats`**; retain parallel raw **`cisco:ndfc:imagemanagement`** because **`validateImage`** payloads vary across **NDFC** maintenance bundles.

references.title: Cisco NDFC LAN Fabric Configuration Guide — Image Management (Release 12.4)
Cisco Nexus 9000 NX-OS Software Upgrade and ISSU compatibility matrix guidance
Cisco DC Networking Application for Splunk (Splunkbase 7777)
Cisco Nexus Dashboard Insights API Reference — fabric/site endpoints
Cisco NDFC REST API Reference (Developer Hub)

knownFalsePositives:
**New-fabric stand-up** leaves **image_policy_compliance** at NA until templates bind **imagePolicy**—Splunk shows a transient gap while inventory onboarding completes—suppress policy_gap macros until CAB tickets record controller attachment milestones and the fabric exits bootstrap windows documented by NetOps.

**Vendor qualification labs** run Maintenance trains under CMDB environment=cert so Out-of-Sync versus LTS targets is scheduled while optics and transceiver matrices bake—tie paging to cert_campaign bridges instead of Sev ladders aimed at production fabrics listed in CMDB tier-one fields.

**Cold-standby fabrics** pin prior-release **imagePolicy** rows on border-leaf roles awaiting failover CHG execution—validateImage may pass while target_version differs from production—apply standby_wave lookups so golden_straggler alerts skip failover_ready estates explicitly tagged in ServiceNow.

**CHG-staged ISSU waves** hold installer.dryRun cohorts Out-of-Sync until installer.commit—amber dashboards overlap wave_stage identifiers—pause Sev timers until dry-run completion timestamps align with automation logs referencing policy:upgrade:install receipts captured by collector scripts.

**Controlled DR drills** pair matched running_version tuples across mirrored racks even when fabric_compliance_pct differs—annotate matched_pair in ServiceNow so vpc_peer_drift macros ignore deliberate symmetry rehearsals tied to failover exercises rather than rogue drift.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Nexus Dashboard clusters running **NDFC 12.4.x** with the Image Management application licensed—scale masters and workers per Cisco worksheets before onboarding **VXLAN EVPN** fabrics whose fabric templates attach **leaf-image**, **spine-image**, and **border-leaf-image** targets through **imagePolicy** references.
• Mint ND bearer tokens (**LDAP**, **SAML**, or local RBAC) for HTTPS collectors calling GET `https://<nd-cluster>/appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/imagemanagement/imagepolicies`—rotate Heavy Forwarder stored secrets quarterly; never paste operator passwords into SPL.
• Install **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (Splunkbase **1546**) or equivalent Python pollers targeting `…/imagemanagement/policies/{policyId}/devices`, `…/lan-fabric/rest/control/fabrics/{fabric}/inventory`, `…/imagemanagement/imageupgrades`, plus optional validateImage probes—keep intervals between **60–300** seconds so controller APIs stay below HTTP **429** thresholds during wide ISSU waves.
• Deploy **cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk** (Splunkbase **7777**) so packaged tiles and your saved searches agree on **cisco:ndfc:imagemanagement** and **cisco:ndfc:fabric** sourcetypes.
• Terminate **facility=local6** syslog bearing **IMAGE-MGMT-INFO**/**IMAGE-MGMT-FAIL** strings through Splunk Connect for Syslog with **Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus** (Splunkbase **6876**) parsing—preserve timestamps across HA forwarders.
• Land data in **index=cisco_dc** (or governance-approved shards), stamp **fabric_name** at ingest, and document index permissions for fabric operators before publishing dashboards.

Step 1 — Data sources
Anchor observability on Software Image Management workflows rather than stray inventory snapshots. Poll GET `/appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/imagemanagement/imagepolicies` to enumerate **imagePolicy** JSON objects—policy identifiers, permitted NX-OS trains (**LTS**, **Maintenance**, **Long-Term-Support** families), template bindings, and default golden selections for spine versus leaf roles. Follow with GET `…/policies/{policyId}/devices` to retrieve **image_policy_compliance** enums (**In-Sync**, **Out-of-Sync**, Failed, NA) alongside **running_version**, **image_policy_target_version**, and timestamps that anchor drift timelines. Mirror fabric scope using GET `/appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/lan-fabric/rest/control/fabrics/{fabric}/inventory` ingested as **cisco:ndfc:fabric**, capturing **serialNumber**, **switchRole**, **vpcPeer** hints, and mgmt addressing so peer-pair comparisons remain authoritative. Capture upgrade choreography through GET `/appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/imagemanagement/imageupgrades` every **60–300** seconds—this stream reveals **installer.dryRun**, **installer.commit**, rollback transitions, installer durations feeding **image_push_duration_seconds**, and identifiers that align with **policy:upgrade:install** automation topics when Cisco exposes them. Subscribe NDI GET `https://<nd-cluster>/sedgeapi/v1/site_status/imageManagement` into **cisco:ndi:health** for summarized posture—treat it as a cross-check layer, not the sole compliance oracle. Combine syslog (**facility=local6**) lines tagged **IMAGE-MGMT-*** MNEMONICs with deduped push_fail counters so benign retries never masquerade as sustained failures. Optional **gRPC** **image-version** telemetry closes gaps when HTTPS polls miss ISSU reload boundaries on **Nexus 93180YC-FX**, **9332C**, **9504**, and **9508** roles.

Volume planning: expect poll payloads to grow with fabric size—budget Heavy Forwarder CPU for gzip decoding and JSON parsing; shard indexes per region if multi-continent dashboards must stay responsive.

Step 2 — Field extractions
Standardize transforms so macros stay portable across **NDFC** maintenance bundles: extract **device_name**, **fabric_name**, **device_role** (**leaf**, **spine**, **border-leaf**), **image_policy_name**, **image_policy_target_version**, **running_version**, **compliance_state**, **last_upgrade_ts**, **pre_upgrade_validation_status**, **pre_issu_compatibility_status**, **post_issu_verification_status**, **image_push_duration_seconds**, **installer_step** (**dryRun**, **commit**, **rollback**), **tmg_compatibility_score**, **vpc_peer**, **peer_switch**, and derived **fabric_compliance_pct**. Strip marketing parentheses from **running_version** strings before comparing against TMG compatibility tuples or PSIRT lookups. Where validateImage payloads nest warnings, flatten WARN/FAIL codes into searchable fields for joins with upgrade jobs.

Step 3 — Initial validation
Validate Splunk outputs against three planes: **NDFC** GUI Image Management (policy assignments, device tables, validateImage banners), **NDI** fabric widgets scoped to image posture, Cisco TMG guidance for **Nexus 9000** upgrades, and spot NX-OS CLI **show install all status** captures referenced in active CHG tasks. Resolve discrepancies involving clock skew on forwarders, partial pagination on large fabrics, or stale snapshots before trusting automated paging.

Step 4 — SPL building blocks
Publish modular SPL fragments: **fabric_compliance_pct** time series (ratio of **In-Sync** devices to polled devices per **fabric_name**), **Out-of-Sync** straggler panels segmented by **device_role**, ISSU wave trackers plotting **installer_step** transitions with ETA estimates based on rolling **image_push_duration_seconds**, weekly **image_push_fail_events** rates normalized per fabric, **vpc_peer_drift** joins comparing paired leaf images within the same fabric, and TMG correlation panels tying low **tmg_compatibility_score** readings to validateImage WARN/FAIL codes.

Step 5 — Comprehensive enrichment patterns
Deepen signal beyond raw compliance enums: correlate **cisco:ndi:health** summaries when fabric dashboards amber ahead of commits; nightly openVuln pulls keyed on **running_version** tuples spotlight CSC identifiers (**CSCwa***, **CSCvy***) intersecting Maintenance trains slated for golden uplift; refresh TMG CSV snapshots whenever Cisco publishes NX-OS compatibility updates for modular **9504**/**9508** platforms; bind ServiceNow CHG bridges carrying wave identifiers referenced inside **policy:upgrade:install** automation logs; attach Cisco TAC **SR** numbers when Failed rows linger across multiple polls; elevate border-leaf mismatches using CMDB **asset_criticality** weights; blend pre-upgrade CPU, CRC, and **CoPP** baselines to distinguish hardware faults from policy drift; run post-ISSU BGP, **vPC**, and ARP soak macros before declaring success; align Splunk ITSI maintenance calendars with CAB freezes; hunt MITRE **T1542.005** narratives where pushes occur without matching CHG; compute blast-radius scores multiplying Out-of-Sync counts by criticality ahead of **installer.commit** storms.

Governance packaging: maintain Git-reviewed FIELDALIAS bundles tied to **NDFC** release notes so camelCase drift during upgrades does not silently break macros; archive baseline screenshots whenever Splunk disagrees with Image Management GUIs so auditors inherit reproducible evidence trails.

Step 6 — Operational runbook
Paging: **Sev-3** when **Out-of-Sync** persists beyond **24h** without approved suppression tied to staged dry-runs; **Sev-2** when ISSU stalls after **installer.commit** or clusters of **IMAGE-MGMT-FAIL** arcs appear; **Sev-1** when coordinated rollback floods span multiple devices inside one **fabric_name** hour. Track fabric-level **MTBF** between controller-reported Failed bursts and **MTTR** from acknowledgement through successful validateImage reruns documented in ServiceNow. Dashboard Studio layouts must honor **fabric_name** tokens for isolation; archive weekly CSV extracts covering Inventory and Updates perspectives for auditors; rehearse escalation quarterly with NetOps and CAB leads.

Troubleshooting cues: persistent NA rows usually mean policies never attached—verify fabric-template bindings before blaming switches; recurring HTTP **401** responses indicate bearer token drift—rotate automation credentials and replay curl probes from the Heavy Forwarder; sudden drops in poll volume often trace to ND cluster patching—coordinate maintenance bridges before declaring fabric-wide compliance gains that are actually collector outages.


Collector reliability and pagination
Large fabrics paginate inventory responses—implement looped REST clients honoring Cisco page tokens so Splunk never drops trailing switches from compliance denominators. When Image Management emits new JSON keys between minor releases, stage FIELDALIAS updates inside a lower environment index before rolling forwarders resume nightly audits.

Audit evidence packs
Quarterly evidence bundles should include saved-search definitions, representative Image Management screenshots, TMG excerpts referenced during validations, ServiceNow CHG identifiers tied to ISSU waves, and CSV extracts proving Splunk dashboards mirrored controller posture at quarter close—attach Splunk revision IDs whenever macros change.

Cross-team rehearsal cadence
Coordinate NetOps with virtualization and storage peers quarterly because ISSU failures occasionally cascade from upstream dependencies rather than NX-OS binaries alone—document rollback rehearsals referencing peer-link checks whenever vpc_peer_drift macros remain red post-CHG closure.

Sampling methodology for giant fleets
Where fabrics exceed practical poll cycles, stratify sampling—always include border-leaf tiers fully, randomly rotate spine subsets per day while maintaining statistical confidence—never omit entire fabrics from denominators unless CAB-approved maintenance explicitly excludes those switches.

Documentation hygiene
Maintain architecture notes stating why controller-bound policies supersede spreadsheet governance for golden firmware—future reviewers inherit rationale whenever procurement spreadsheets disagree with Image Management telemetry archived inside Splunk.

Closing reminder: tie Splunk RBAC roles to fabric tenancy so tenant-specific dashboards cannot pivot across unrelated Image Management policies.
grandmaExplanation: We check that the Cisco fabric controller’s golden firmware rules match what each switch actually runs, and we raise a hand before mixed software versions spoil planned upgrade nights or surprise reloads.
